title: Exact and Approximate Nearest-Neighbor Indexes · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers exact and approximate nearest-neighbor indexes into a new ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of ANN recall, latency, and resource experiment with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.
Explain and tune HNSW construction, search breadth, graph degree, recall, latency, memory, updates, and deletion behavior.
Explain coarse partitioning, probes, product or scalar quantization, training data, compression loss, and rebuild requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Exact Vector Baseline
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 6069 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare exact, HNSW, IVF, and quantized indexes with recall, latency, memory, and filter evidence; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build an exact flat-search oracle on a bounded corpus for correctness, recall comparison, and changed-filter tests.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Exact Vector Baseline.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Ann Capacity Selection
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-ann-capacity-selection-xwm964" that uses case 1864 to compare exact, HNSW, IVF, and quantized indexes with recall, latency, memory, and filter evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Ann Capacity Selection.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Ann Capacity Selection into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Ann Capacity Selection, keep Rag Ann Capacity Selection connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-ann-capacity-selection-xwm964
# Competency: rag-ann-capacity-selection.
# Case 1864: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_ann_capacity_selection_xwm964(vectors: int = 10000, dimensions: int = 384, exact_recall: float = 0.96, latency_ms: float = 18.0, memory_mb: int = 96, filtered: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xwm964 = "xwm964-469mwx-1864"
    assert evidence_variant_xwm964
    evidence_axes_xwm964 = (
        "corpusxwm964",
        "query469mwx",
        "tenant1864",
        "indexxwm9641864",
        "prompt469mwx1864",
        "evaluation1864xwm964",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xwm964)) == 6
    if vectors <= 0 or dimensions <= 0:
        return False, 0.0, "index-shape"
    if not 0.0 <= exact_recall <= 1.0:
        return False, exact_recall, "recall-range"
    if latency_ms <= 0.0 or memory_mb <= 0:
        return False, latency_ms, "resource-measurement"
    if not filtered:
        return False, exact_recall, "filter-recall"
    utility = exact_recall / latency_ms
    assert utility > 0.0
    return True, round(utility, 5), "ann-tradeoff"
